title: Displays, Cameras, Audio, and Accessible Output · Device Forensics
summary: A realistic displays, cameras, audio, and accessible output ticket contains one plausible but causally wrong diagnosis or unsafe action. Find the first unsupported inference and repair the evidence chain. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in accessible audiovisual signal-path diagnosis and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Compare LCD panel types, OLED, projectors, digitizers, backlights, inverters, touch layers, privacy filters, and failure signatures.
Relate physical size, pixel resolution, density, scaling, refresh, orientation, reflow, zoom, and readable workspace to the user task.
Trace source, GPU, port, cable, adapter, dock, sink, input selection, negotiation, power, and operating-system state before replacing parts.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# comptia-a-plus cumulative fictional support evidence portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic text-contract review only. They never execute learner
# commands, inspect the host, touch devices or accounts, connect to networks, open equipment,
# handle malware, erase media, contact people, or claim certification.
workspace: support
ticket-id: fictional-ticket
user-impact: [replace with user outcome, scope, severity, and urgency]
environment: [replace with asset, version, configuration, owner, dependency, and evidence time]
safety-authority: [replace with consent, hazards, data custody, privacy, access, and stop rules]
baseline: [replace with expected behavior, known-good state, conditions, and measurement]
symptom: [replace with observed behavior, reproduction, frequency, timing, and evidence]
recent-change: [replace with timeline, relevant changes, counterevidence, and unknowns]
hypothesis: [replace with falsifiable cause, competing explanation, prediction, and confidence]
test: [replace with one safe reversible discriminating test, changed case, and stop rule]
observation: [replace with actual result, provenance, rejected inference, and next decision]
repair-rollback: [replace with least-change repair, authority, backup, trigger, and restored state]
verify: [replace with user outcome, original, changed, side-effect, restart, and recurrence checks]
prevention: [replace with prevention, monitoring, owner, review date, and residual risk]
escalation: [replace with stop threshold, destination, evidence packet, and requested decision]
documentation: [replace with plain structured status, redaction, access, handoff, and uncertainty]
transfer-boundary: authorized disposable equipment or simulator only
review:

steps.title: CompTIA A+ support Inspect the failure record · Debug Display Technologies
steps.instruction:
debug case — A hybrid classroom has an intermittent external image, echo, camera privacy conflict, unreadable scaling, and color-only status. The debug variation 5 changes the user outcome, device, version, hazard, recent change, symptom, access need, competing cause, and transfer limit. Competency probe: Compare LCD panel types, OLED, projectors, digitizers, backlights, inverters, touch layers, privacy filters, and failure signatures.

Inspect the Display Technologies trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

Evidence target: CompTIA A+ support Inspect the failure record · Debug Display Technologies.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for compare lcd panel types, oled, projectors, digitizers, backlights, inverters, touch layers, privacy filters, and failure signatures. For CompTIA A+ support Inspect the failure record · Debug Display Technologies, keep Display Technologies connected to mechanically reviewable user-impact, asset, safety, baseline, symptom, hypothesis, test, observation, repair, rollback, verification, escalation, documentation, and transfer decision evidence.

content.text: For Display Technologies, distinguish a claim from evidence by locating the relevant precondition, trace or invariant, and changed input result.

stimulus.title: Display Technologies case record
stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: A hybrid classroom has an intermittent external image, echo, camera privacy conflict, unreadable scaling, and color-only status. lines.label: evidence

This does not satisfy Display Technologies for accessible audiovisual signal-path diagnosis.
The record names the assumption, applies Display Technologies, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.

steps.why: Retrieving Accessible Output through the concrete accessible audiovisual signal-path diagnosis case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For CompTIA A+ support Publish the incident record · Debug Accessible Output, keep Accessible Output connected to mechanically reviewable user-impact, asset, safety, baseline, symptom, hypothesis, test, observation, repair, rollback, verification, escalation, documentation, and transfer decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Provide keyboard, zoom, contrast, non-color, caption, transcript, screen-reader, haptic, and alternate-output routes with user verification.

hints: For CompTIA A+ support Publish the incident record · Debug Accessible Output, name the user impact, fictional ticket, asset, environment, authorization, hazard, baseline, symptom, timeline, hypothesis, test, observation, repair, rollback, verification, prevention, escalation, accessible documentation, and transfer boundary, Accessible Output precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At CompTIA A+ support Publish the incident record · Debug Accessible Output, trace this concrete input and predict user impact, safety and authority, baseline, reproduction, timeline, hypothesis, bounded test, observation, rejection, repair, rollback, original and changed-case verification, prevention, escalation, and controlled transfer evidence before reviewing it: A hybrid classroom has an intermittent external image, echo, camera privacy conflict, unreadable scaling, and color-only status. The debug variation 3 changes the user outcome, device, version, hazard, recent change, symptom, access need, competing cause, and transfer limit. Competency probe: Provide keyboard, zoom, contrast, non-color, caption, transcript, screen-reader, haptic, and alternate-output routes with user verification.

For CompTIA A+ support Publish the incident record · Debug Accessible Output, challenge the documented misconception with changed-user, device, version, hazard, baseline, symptom, timeline, access need, hypothesis, test result, repair, restart, recurrence, escalation, and controlled equipment-transfer evidence: Accessibility is complete when automated contrast checks pass.
